MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for further Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Sandy Gail Munmon, a/k/a Sandy Pubill.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #86005. Email: mtorreano@sftc.org.

Darren Orr, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Gloria Munmon, who was placed under oath, but did not testify.
Dr. Aazaz Ul Haq, a witness for plaintiff, was placed under oath, but did not testify.
Defendant Sandy Gail Munmon, a/k/a Sandy Pubill, was placed under oath, but did not testify.
Counsel, the parties and the witness all appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.

Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint for Resulting Trust, Fraud/Intentional Misrepresentation, Fraud/Negligent Misrepresentation, Statutory Elder Abuse, Conversion and Breach of Fiduciary Duty was filed on October 26, 2020.
Order Granting Plaintiff's Motion for Terminating Sanctions for Disobeiance of prior Court Orders was issued on December 31, 2020.
Plaintiff's Statement of Damages was served on defendant Sandy Gail Munmon, a/k/a Sandy Pubill, on November 6, 2020 and filed with the Clerk's Office on February 2, 2021.
Default as to defendant Sandy Gail Munmon, a/k/a Sandy Pubill, was entered on February 2, 2021.
DOE defendants were dismissed on December 11, 2020.

While not ruling on the merits of plaintiff's request for entry of Court Judgment, the Court DENIED plaintiff's application to file documents under seal, and instructed Mr. Orr to submit a declaration enumerating the hours worked in this matter and the tasks performed in support of the request for entry of Court Judgment.

Additionally, the Court informed defendant Sandy Gail Munmon, a/k/a Sandy Pubill, that her most recent submission of her request to set-aside the default entered February 2, 2021 had been rejected by the Clerk's Office.

Matter is CONTINUED to Thursday, June 24, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514 for further hearing on plaintiff Gloria Munmon's request for entry of Court Judgment. No further notice is required. The parties waive notice of the June 24th hearing date.
